title: Anil setiflora
intro: Anil setiflora, also known as Indian milk bush or arni, is a plant species belonging to the family Asclepiadaceae, native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as wound healing, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Anil setiflora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Anil setiflora is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin infections and respiratory issues.

This family is a part of the larger order Sapindales, which also includes other families such as Sapindaceae and Meliaceae.
